title: edit_file cross-platform line-counting may misreport diff stats for CRLF and unicode
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Functional Correctness

## Location
src/tools/file/edit.rs:91-92 (lines_removed/lines_added counts)

## Violation
```rust
let lines_removed = u32::try_from(old_string.lines().count()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX);
let lines_added = u32::try_from(new_string.lines().count()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX);
```

`str::lines()` recognizes only `\n` and `\r\n` as line terminators. On a file with `\r`-only (old Mac) endings, both counts are 1 regardless of content. Single line containing no terminator yields count 1, empty string yields count 0 — so adding a single line of "x" reports lines_added=1, lines_removed=0; replacing "x\n" with "y\n" reports lines_added=1, lines_removed=1 (correct). But replacing "x\n" with "y" reports lines_added=1, lines_removed=1 — a SUBTRACTION of a newline is invisible to the diff stats.

The guardrails::record_file_modification is then fed misleading values, and check_diff_thresholds may fire (or not) based on the wrong numbers. Same pattern is in notebook.rs:253-256 with the same issue.

## Mandated refactor
Use `bytecount::count(s.as_bytes(), b'\n')` for an accurate physical-newline count, with explicit handling for files ending without a newline. Or count `s.matches('\n').count() + (if s.is_empty() || s.ends_with('\n') { 0 } else { 1 })`. Document the chosen semantics.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
